chapter: Chapter 5: The Fundamental Unit of Life

questions.question: Why are mitochondria called semi-autonomous organelles?
questions.answer: Because they contain their own DNA and ribosomes.

questions.question: Why are lysosomes called suicide bags?
questions.answer: They contain enzymes that can digest the cell itself.

questions.question: Why is the plasma membrane called selectively permeable?
questions.answer: Because it allows only certain substances to pass through.

questions.question: Why do plant cells have large vacuoles?
questions.answer: Large vacuoles maintain turgidity and store substances.

questions.question: Why do muscle cells contain many mitochondria?
questions.answer: Muscle cells require large amounts of energy.

questions.question: Why are ribosomes called protein factories?
questions.answer: Because they synthesize proteins in cells.

questions.question: Why do plant cells have a cell wall?
questions.answer: To provide mechanical support and protection.

questions.question: Why do prokaryotic cells lack a nucleus?
questions.answer: Because their genetic material is not enclosed by a membrane.

questions.question: Why is diffusion important in cells?
questions.answer: It helps exchange gases like oxygen and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: Why is osmosis important in plants?
questions.answer: It helps roots absorb water from soil.

questions.question: Why is the nucleus called the control center of the cell?
questions.answer: Because it regulates cell activities and stores genetic information.

questions.question: Why are plastids important for plants?
questions.answer: They store food and help in photosynthesis.

questions.question: Why is the ER called the transport system of the cell?
questions.answer: Because it forms channels for movement of materials.

questions.question: Why are chromosomes important?
questions.answer: They carry genes responsible for heredity.

questions.question: Why do plant cells perform photosynthesis?
questions.answer: Because chloroplasts contain chlorophyll for capturing light energy.

questions.question: Why is mitosis important for growth?
questions.answer: It produces identical cells needed for growth and repair.

questions.question: Why is meiosis important for reproduction?
questions.answer: It produces gametes with half the chromosome number.

questions.question: Why are chloroplasts green?
questions.answer: Because they contain chlorophyll pigment.

questions.question: Why do animal cells lack a cell wall?
questions.answer: Because flexibility is needed for movement.

questions.question: Why are vacuoles smaller in animal cells?
questions.answer: Because storage functions are less extensive than in plants.

questions.question: Why is DNA important in living organisms?
questions.answer: It stores hereditary information.

questions.question: Why are cells considered the basic unit of life?
questions.answer: Because all life processes occur within cells.

questions.question: Why do cells divide?
questions.answer: Cells divide for growth, repair and reproduction.

questions.question: Why is the Golgi apparatus important?
questions.answer: It modifies and packages proteins for transport.

questions.question: Why are enzymes important in lysosomes?
questions.answer: They break down wastes and damaged cell parts.
